dataset.description: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.

columns.description: Customer's contact number stored as integer (12-digit format)
columns.name: phone

columns.description: Multi-line physical address including street details
columns.name: address

columns.description: Lifetime count of orders placed by customer (range:1-20)
columns.name: total_orders

columns.description: Foreign key linking to orders table
columns.name: order_id

columns.description: Foreign key linking to customers table
columns.name: customer_id

samples: Nothing to complain about, but nothing exceptional.
Delivery was fine, but the product could be better.

columns.description: Difference between promised and actual delivery time (may be negative for early deliveries)
columns.name: delivery_time_minutes

columns.description: Causal factor for delays (currently only 'Traffic' in samples)
columns.name: reasons_if_delayed

columns.description: Foreign key linking to products table
columns.name: product_id

columns.description: Foreign key linking to products table
columns.name: product_id

columns.description: Monthly total of damaged items (mostly 0 with rare exceptions)
columns.name: damaged_stock

columns.description: Number of times campaign was displayed
columns.name: impressions

columns.description: Performance status matching delivery performance table
columns.name: delivery_status

columns.description: Foreign key linking to orders table
columns.name: order_id

columns.description: Foreign key linking to products table
columns.name: product_id

columns.description: Price per unit at time of purchase
columns.name: unit_price

desc: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.
goal: Perform a statistical analysis of sales volumes, revenue contributions, and price sensitivity across different products or product categories. This descriptive approach identifies key revenue drivers and top-selling items, using visual tools such as bar charts and pie charts to highlight performance differences, which in turn supports decisions related to pricing strategies, inventory management, and promotional activities.
name: Visual Analytics System blinkit_goal3_1742637072

plan.path: The user starts with View 1 at the Overview level to see sales patterns across product categories and time segments. Category selection triggers updates in Views 2-5. View 2 (Exploration) reveals price-quantity relationships, View 3 (Exploration) compares revenue metrics, View 4 (Insight) shows inventory-sales alignment, and View 5 (Insight) detects pricing anomalies. Interactions flow from category selection to all subordinate views, enabling progressive refinement from patterns to actionable insights.

effect.description: Filters subsequent views by selected product categories

visualization.description: Stacked bar chart showing total sales quantity per category with time-of-day segments
Faceted scatterplots showing price-quantity relationships per category with revenue bubbles

effect.description: Changes encoding calculation between sum(order_total) and sum(order_total*margin_percentage)

visualization.description: Toggleable bar chart showing raw revenue vs margin-weighted revenue
Dual-axis temporal alignment of inventory vs sales
Histogram of price ratios with anomaly thresholds marked

data.reason: Current I1 interaction requires propagating product categories to customer segment analysis but original task 4 only joins customers+orders
data.requirement: Ensure all joined tables explicitly include product_category in customer segmentation views through orders->order_items->products relationships

interaction.reason: blinkit_inventory.date format (DD-MM-YYYY) mismatches orders' timestamps, risking misleading correlations without first harmonizing time dimensions
interaction.requirement: Category brush propagates EXCEPT to inventory views until temporal alignment achieved

visualization.reason: Original stacked time segments (morning/afternoon/evening) don't align with timestamp format in blinkit_orders.order_date (full datetime). Time slider maintains temporal context without overloading categories.
visualization.requirement: Bar chart showing sales frequency by product category with time-slider-based temporal filtering (instead of stacking)

visualization.reason: Original grouped bar + IQR error bars misrepresent continuous distribution. Box plots better show spread despite non-interactivity constraints when paired with pre-calculated quartiles in SQL
visualization.requirement: Box plot with visible quartiles for order_total distribution per customer_segment

sqls.SQL: SELECT category, quantity, order_date FROM blinkit_products JOIN blinkit_order_items ON blinkit_products.product_id = blinkit_order_items.product_id JOIN blinkit_orders ON blinkit_order_items.order_id = blinkit_orders.order_id
sqls.name: sales_summary

sqls.SQL: SELECT category, quantity, unit_price FROM blinkit_products JOIN blinkit_order_items ON blinkit_products.product_id = blinkit_order_items.product_id
sqls.name: price_quantity_analysis

sqls.SQL: SELECT category, order_total, margin_percentage FROM blinkit_products JOIN blinkit_orders ON blinkit_products.product_id = blinkit_orders.product_id
sqls.name: revenue_analysis

sqls.SQL: SELECT stock_received, quantity, order_date FROM blinkit_inventory JOIN blinkit_order_items ON blinkit_inventory.product_id = blinkit_order_items.product_id JOIN blinkit_orders ON blinkit_order_items.order_id = blinkit_orders.order_id
sqls.name: inventory_sales_alignment

sqls.SQL: SELECT unit_price, mrp, category FROM blinkit_products
sqls.name: price_ratio_analysis

tasks.method: Stacked bar chart with product categories on X-axis and total sales quantity on Y-axis. Time-of-day patterns represented as color-stacked segments (e.g., morning/afternoon/evening) derived from order_date timestamps. Linked to I1 for category brushing.
tasks.objective: Identify top-selling categories and their temporal sales patterns to optimize inventory allocation and delivery scheduling

tasks.method: Toggleable bar chart (per I2) showing category-level revenue sum (primary mode) vs margin-weighted revenue calculated as ∑(order_total × margin_percentage). Color encodes performance quartiles relative to selected metric.
tasks.objective: Compare raw revenue contribution vs profitability-adjusted performance across product categories

tasks.method: Scatterplot matrix with price-quantity pairs per category, trend lines showing elasticity. Bubble size represents total revenue (price×quantity). Category brush (I1) filters matrix subplots.
tasks.objective: Analyze price sensitivity and demand elasticity patterns across product categories

tasks.method: Grouped bar chart (V4) with customer segments on X-axis and error bars showing ±1.5 IQR ranges. Bar height represents mean order value. Click interactions filter co-occurring views via shared segment parameter.
tasks.objective: Compare spending patterns across customer segments to inform loyalty program adjustments

tasks.method: Line chart overlay: x-axis=time, y1=stock received (inventory), y2=sales quantity (orders). Category brush (I1) highlights corresponding lines. Anomalies detected via stock-out events (y2 > y1).
tasks.objective: Identify inventory replenishment needs through temporal alignment of stock levels and sales volume

tasks.method: Histogram with price ratios (unit_price/mrp) across categories. Anomaly thresholds marked at <0.85 and >1.0 ratios. Category brush (I1) enables focused outlier inspection.
tasks.objective: Detect pricing irregularities and potential MAP policy violations
